title: Hara Bhara Kebab (Green Veggie Patties)
servings: makes about 12-15 medium-sized kebabs, which typically serves 4 people as an appetizer

instruction.title: Prep Greens & Peas:
steps: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 2 minutes, then immediately transfer to ice-cold water to retain color. Drain and chop finely. Lightly mash boiled green peas.

instruction.title: Toast Cashews:
steps: In a dry pan, lightly toast the halved cashew nuts over medium heat until golden and fragrant. Set aside.

instruction.title: Mix Veggies:
steps: In a large bowl, combine chopped spinach, mashed peas, boiled mashed potato, grated paneer, and half of the toasted cashew nuts (finely chopped).

instruction.title: Add Flavors:
steps: Add chopped coriander, green chilies, ginger, garlic, chaat masala, garam masala, cumin powder, amchur powder, and salt. Mix well.

instruction.title: Bind the Mixture:
steps: Add cornflour and bread crumbs. Mix thoroughly until the mixture holds together. Adjust bread crumbs if necessary.

instruction.title: Shape Kebabs:
steps: Take small portions and shape into patties (2-3 inches diameter, 1/2 inch thick). Press 2-3 toasted cashew halves on top of each kebab for garnish and crunch.

instruction.title: Cook Kebabs:
steps: Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Shallow fry the kebabs until golden and crisp on both sides (about 3-4 minutes each side). Drain on paper towels.

instruction.title: Serve:
steps: Serve hot with coriander-mint chutney and lemon wedges.
Ensure spinach is well-drained before mixing to avoid soggy kebabs.
You can bake the kebabs at 375°F (190°C) for 20 minutes for a healthier version, flipping halfway through.
Paneer adds richness but is optional; you can replace with extra mashed potato or peas.
Adjust spices as per your heat tolerance.
